name: Active Scanning
description: Adversaries scan victim IP blocks or services to gather information for targeting.

name: Gather Victim Host Information
description: Adversaries gather details about victim hosts to plan follow-on activity.

name: Acquire Infrastructure
description: Adversaries buy, lease, or rent infrastructure for use throughout the attack lifecycle.

name: Obtain Capabilities
description: Adversaries buy or steal capabilities such as malware, exploits, or certificates.

name: Phishing
description: Adversaries send phishing messages to gain access to victim systems.

name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
description: Adversaries exploit a weakness in an internet-facing host or application.

name: Valid Accounts
description: Adversaries use compromised credentials to bypass access controls.

name: External Remote Services
description: Adversaries abuse external remote services (VPN, Citrix, RDP gateways) to gain initial access.

name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
description: Adversaries abuse command/script interpreters to execute commands or payloads.

name: PowerShell
description: Adversaries abuse PowerShell for execution, discovery, and lateral movement.

name: Exploitation for Client Execution
description: Adversaries exploit software vulnerabilities in client applications to execute code.

name: User Execution
description: Adversaries rely on a user to execute a malicious file or link.

name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution
description: Adversaries configure system settings to execute a program during system boot or logon.

name: Scheduled Task/Job
description: Adversaries abuse task scheduling to execute code for persistence or execution.

name: Create Account
description: Adversaries create accounts to maintain access to victim systems.

name: Account Manipulation
description: Adversaries manipulate accounts to maintain access, e.g. adding credentials or permissions.

name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
description: Adversaries exploit software vulnerabilities to elevate privileges.

name: Process Injection
description: Adversaries inject code into processes to evade defenses and/or elevate privileges.

name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism
description: Adversaries bypass mechanisms designed to control elevated privileges (e.g. UAC).

name: Indicator Removal
description: Adversaries delete or alter artifacts to remove evidence of their presence.

name: Obfuscated Files or Information
description: Adversaries obfuscate content to make it difficult to discover or analyze.

name: Masquerading
description: Adversaries manipulate features of artifacts to make them appear legitimate.

name: Impair Defenses
description: Adversaries disable or modify security tools to avoid detection.

name: Brute Force
description: Adversaries use brute force techniques to gain access to accounts.

name: Password Guessing
description: Adversaries guess passwords without prior knowledge of leaked/plaintext credentials.

name: OS Credential Dumping
description: Adversaries dump credentials from the OS to obtain account login information.

name: Unsecured Credentials
description: Adversaries search for insecurely stored credentials such as config files or scripts.

name: Modify Authentication Process
description: Adversaries modify authentication mechanisms to access credentials or bypass MFA.

name: System Information Discovery
description: Adversaries gather detailed information about the OS and hardware configuration.

name: Account Discovery
description: Adversaries enumerate local or domain accounts on a system.

name: Remote System Discovery
description: Adversaries enumerate other systems on a network for lateral movement targeting.

name: Network Service Discovery
description: Adversaries scan for network services running on remote hosts.

name: Remote Services
description: Adversaries use valid accounts to log into services designed for remote access.

name: Remote Desktop Protocol
description: Adversaries use RDP to move laterally within a network.

name: Use Alternate Authentication Material
description: Adversaries use stolen tokens, hashes, or tickets to bypass normal auth.

name: Data from Local System
description: Adversaries search local system sources to find files of interest.

name: Email Collection
description: Adversaries target user email to collect sensitive information.

name: Archive Collected Data
description: Adversaries compress and/or encrypt collected data prior to exfiltration.

name: Application Layer Protocol
description: Adversaries use application-layer protocols to blend C2 traffic with legitimate traffic.

name: Ingress Tool Transfer
description: Adversaries transfer tools or files from an external system into a compromised environment.

name: Encrypted Channel
description: Adversaries employ encryption to conceal C2 traffic from network defenses.

name: Proxy
description: Adversaries use a connection proxy to direct C2 traffic and mask its true source.

name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
description: Adversaries steal data by exfiltrating it over an existing C2 channel.

name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
description: Adversaries exfiltrate data using a protocol different from the existing C2 channel.

name: Data Encrypted for Impact
description: Adversaries encrypt data to interrupt availability, e.g. ransomware.

name: Inhibit System Recovery
description: Adversaries delete or disable OS recovery features to prevent restoration.

name: Service Stop
description: Adversaries stop or disable services to render systems unavailable.

name: Network Denial of Service
description: Adversaries degrade or block availability of targeted resources to users.
